summary: Web-based medical records tracker that stores CBC, LFT, Cholesterol, Sugar, BP, and general ailment records tagged with dates. Builds a historical timeline and uses AI to analyze health metric progression over time. Supports user login with separate user consoles and a persistent left-side dashboard panel shown when data is available. Supports family member profiles (up to 8 per user); uploaded reports auto-identify patient name and date, and all extracted tests are added under the correct family member profile.
architectural_notes: Web app built with React + Motoko (not Android)
Medical records stored with date metadata for historical tracking
User authentication required; each user has an isolated console/data view
Left-side dashboard panel is conditionally shown based on user data availability
OCR feature required for PDF/image uploads to extract dates, test names, and test results
Each user can have up to 8 family member profiles; all medical records are scoped to a family member, not just the principal
known_issues: OCR extraction fails to detect/parse the date from uploaded medical report documents

existing_features.title: Build a medical record entry form UI that allows users to select a record category (CBC, LFT, Cholesterol, Blood Sugar, Blood Pressure, General Ailment), fill in the relevant metric fields, and submit with a date. The form must dynamically display the correct input fields based on the selected category.

Build a historical timeline view that displays all past medical records in reverse chronological order. Records should be groupable and filterable by category. Each entry should show the date, category, and key metric values at a glance.

Build a progression analysis dashboard that charts health metric trends over time using available historical data. For each category with multiple data points, display a line chart showing how values have changed across dates. Include a summary section that highlights notable trends such as improving, worsening, or stable metrics.

Design and apply a clean, modern medical-grade visual theme across the entire app. Use a calm, clinical aesthetic with a white and soft teal/green palette, clear typography, and card-based layouts. Navigation should provide easy access to: Add Record, Timeline, and Analysis views.

Integrate Internet Identity authentication so that each user must log in before accessing any medical records. On the backend, scope all record storage and retrieval operations by the caller's principal, so each user only sees and manages their own records. Update the add, getAll, and delete endpoints to use `msg.caller` as the user key.

Build a login/landing screen that is shown to unauthenticated users. It should display the MediTrack branding, a brief description of the app, and a prominent 'Login with Internet Identity' button. Authenticated users must be redirected away from this screen to the main app layout.

Redesign the main app layout to include a persistent left-side dashboard panel (sidebar) that is only rendered when the authenticated user has at least one medical record saved. The sidebar should display a compact summary of the user's health data: record counts per category, most recent entry date per category, and quick-navigation links to the Timeline and Analysis pages. The existing top navigation should remain for primary page routing. When no records exist, the sidebar is hidden and the main content area spans full width.

Add a user identity indicator in the top navigation bar for authenticated users. Display a truncated version of the user's principal (or a generic avatar icon) and a 'Logout' button or menu that calls the Internet Identity logout function and returns the user to the login screen.

Add an 'Upload Record' option to the Add Record page UI. The upload flow must allow the user to select or drag-and-drop a PDF or image file, display a preview or filename confirmation, trigger OCR extraction on the uploaded file, and pre-populate the medical record form fields (date, category, and metric values) with the extracted data for user review and correction before submission.

Extend the OCR extraction logic (frontend/src/lib/ocrExtraction.ts) to also detect the patient name from uploaded report text. Add regex patterns that match common report header formats (e.g., 'Patient Name:', 'Name:', 'Patient:') and return the extracted name alongside the existing date and test result fields.

Extend the OCR extraction logic to extract ALL detected test categories and their metric values from a single uploaded report, not just the first matched category. The extraction result must return an array of record objects, each containing a category, its metric fields, and the shared report date, so that a multi-panel report (e.g., one file containing both CBC and LFT results) produces multiple records.

Update the UploadRecordForm component (frontend/src/components/UploadRecordForm.tsx) to: (1) after OCR extraction display the detected patient name and allow the user to confirm or change it via a dropdown/select of existing family member profiles plus an option to create a new profile; (2) display all extracted test categories as separate pending records (one card or row per category); (3) allow the user to review and edit each record's fields before submission; (4) on submit, add every extracted record under the selected/confirmed family member profile by calling the familyMemberId-scoped addRecord endpoint for each record.

Add a Family Members management UI accessible from the main navigation or settings. It must allow the authenticated user to view all existing family member profiles (name, record count), add a new family member (name input), and delete a family member profile with a confirmation dialog. Enforce the 8-member limit with an inline error message when the limit is reached. The manual Add Record form must also include a family member selector so records can be manually entered for any family member.

Fix the date extraction logic in `frontend/src/lib/ocrExtraction.ts` so that the report date is reliably detected from uploaded medical report documents. Expand the regex patterns to cover common date formats found in medical reports, including but not limited to: 'DD/MM/YYYY', 'MM/DD/YYYY', 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'DD-MM-YYYY', 'DD MMM YYYY' (e.g., '12 Jan 2024'), 'MMM DD, YYYY' (e.g., 'Jan 12, 2024'), and label-prefixed formats such as 'Date:', 'Report Date:', 'Collection Date:', 'Sample Date:', 'Test Date:', and 'Date of Report:'. The extracted date must be returned as a valid ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD) in the `ExtractedTestData` result and pre-populated in the date field of each extracted record displayed in the `UploadRecordForm` component.

new_feature_requests.summary: Medical record entry for CBC, LFT, Cholesterol, Sugar, BP, and general ailments with date tagging
Historical timeline view of all medical records
AI-powered analysis of health metric progression over time

Create a backend data model and API to store medical records with the following categories: CBC (Complete Blood Count), LFT (Liver Function Test), Cholesterol, Blood Sugar, Blood Pressure, and General Ailments. Each record must be tagged with a date and record type. Support create, read, and delete operations.
User login and authentication with separate user consoles (each user sees only their own records)
Persistent left-side dashboard panel that appears when the logged-in user has data available
Upload medical records as PDF or image files; use OCR to extract dates, test names, and test results automatically
Add a backend endpoint that accepts an uploaded file (PDF or image encoded as binary/base64), stores the raw file data per principal, and returns a stable reference ID for the uploaded asset.

Add a backend OCR processing endpoint that takes a stored file reference ID, performs text extraction on the file content, and returns structured fields: detected date, test names, and test result values parsed from the extracted text.

Add a `listUploadedFiles` backend endpoint that returns metadata (file ID, upload timestamp, MIME type) for all files uploaded by the calling principal, and a `deleteUploadedFile` endpoint to remove a file by ID.
Support up to 8 family member profiles per user account. Each profile has a name and stores medical records independently. Records are scoped to a specific family member.
When a report is uploaded, OCR must identify the patient name and record date, automatically map the record to the matching family member profile, and add all detected tests/metrics to that profile.

Extend the backend data model to support up to 8 family member profiles per authenticated user. Each family member profile must have a unique ID, a name, and an independent collection of medical records scoped to that profile. Add backend endpoints: createFamilyMember (name) → profile ID, listFamilyMembers → list of {id, name}, deleteFamilyMember (id), and update all existing addRecord, getAllRecords, and deleteRecord endpoints to accept an optional familyMemberId parameter so that records can be stored and retrieved per family member. Records without a familyMemberId continue to be scoped to the caller's own profile.
